excerpt: He created Adam and all the prophets preceding him, each serving as a necessary preparation for his ultimate appearance.
source_location_hint: Discussions of prophetic precedence and the final messenger

excerpt: When I established My Throne upon water, it trembled. But when I inscribed upon it the testimony—'There is no god but Allāh; Muḥammad is the Messenger of Allāh'—it became firm and still. The Throne stands upon water, which symbolizes knowledge.
source_authority: Chief Qadi of the Fatimid Caliphate

excerpt: Rage, earth, and women are the root of all three corruptions.
source_authority: Chief Summoner (Dāʿī al-Duʿāt) of Fatimid Cairo
source_location_hint: Sermons on the nature of human desire and satanic influence

excerpt: O Abū Dharr, conduct yourself in this world as a stranger or as a wayfarer. Consider yourself among the dead. Speak little, but remember Allāh greatly. Through this, you will overcome Satan.
source_authority: Chief Qadi of the Fatimid Caliphate
source_location_hint: Transmitted prophetic counsels on spiritual discipline

excerpt: Fever is a flame from the flames of Hell. Fever is a purification sent by a Merciful Lord. The sword is an eraser of sins.
source_authority: Chief Summoner (Dāʿī al-Duʿāt) of Fatimid Cairo
source_location_hint: Teachings on divine mercy and atonement through affliction

excerpt: I draw near to broken hearts. The slips and errors of the prophets are of this character: as soon as they sought forgiveness, their ranks were elevated and they drew nearer to Allāh.
source_authority: Ismaili philosopher and poet of the Iranian tradition
source_location_hint: Discourse on divine proximity and spiritual perfection

excerpt: What has been shall be again. That which has already occurred will recur in the future and manifest itself anew; there is nothing new beneath this sun.
source_location_hint: Cosmological discussions of temporal cycles and repetition
